record_identity.title: Ambiguity Preservation and Clarification Routing Repair

summary: This patch records a runtime interpretation repair for VIGIL-2026-FM-0023. The repaired failure pattern is not a simple refusal defect. It is the premature collapse of ambiguous, low-confidence, inherited, contextual, or weakly correlated signals into definitive classification, arbitration, safeguard, refusal, or execution-routing states before the ambiguity has been resolved.
why_it_matters_to_CAM: The failure pattern exposes a governance-risk condition where multiple weak signals can converge into a high-confidence runtime outcome without preserving uncertainty, contextual freshness, signal independence, or clarification opportunity. This can create false positive refusals, misaligned safeguard activation, misclassification, inappropriate escalation, premature arbitration convergence, user-facing rationale mismatch, execution-path distortion, or interpretive incoherence. CAM repair therefore needs to preserve ambiguity as an operational state and recognise clarification as a valid runtime and arbitration outcome rather than forcing proceed/refuse convergence.

change_classification.change_scope: Caelestis Architecture / ambiguity preservation / clarification routing / arbitration outcome expansion / refusal-expression integrity / failure taxonomy harmonisation
change_classification.doctrine_amendment_status: implemented in working branch or maintainer workspace; pending canonical main-branch confirmation if not yet merged

changed_components: CAM-BS2025-AEON-003-SCH-04 §2.4 Clarification as an Arbitration Outcome
CAM-EQ2026-OPERATIONS-003-SUP-01 §3.10.2 Weak-Signal Cascade and Ambiguity Collapse Failure
CAM-EQ2026-OPERATIONS-003-SUP-01 §3.8.14 Structural Locality and Hierarchical Placement Failure
change_details.change_summary: Repair 1 — Ambiguity preservation: Added or formalised a runtime mechanism requiring unresolved ambiguity to remain unresolved until sufficient directional confidence exists to support routing, safeguard activation, refusal, or execution-sensitive behaviour. Repair 2 — Clarification routing: Added clarification as a valid arbitration outcome where ambiguity remains material and recoverable, preventing forced proceed/refuse convergence when a question can safely resolve uncertainty. Repair 3 — Ambiguity-aware refusal handling: Added refusal-expression requirements for cases where refusal follows unresolved or partially resolved ambiguity, preventing runtime uncertainty from being narrated as established user intent. Repair 4 — Failure taxonomy harmonisation: Added Weak-Signal Cascade and Ambiguity Collapse Failure and Structural Locality and Hierarchical Placement Failure as material taxonomy classes. Repair 5 — Taxonomy restraint: Declined to add Refusal-Rationale Integrity Failure as a standalone class because the observed refusal wording problem is downstream of ambiguity collapse rather than the primary pathology.
change_details.root_cause: Weak-signal ambiguity collapse. Multiple low-confidence signals were treated as collectively equivalent to a resolved high-confidence classification state without sufficient preservation of uncertainty, contextual freshness, signal independence, or clarification opportunity.
change_details.contributing_cause: Structural locality effects. Governance behaviour may be influenced not only by textual content but also by hierarchical placement and parent-child structural relationships. Misplacement of runtime gates can alter apparent activation pathways, signal inheritance relationships, or safeguard interpretation despite unchanged wording.

taxonomy_entries_added.taxonomy_label: Weak-Signal Cascade and Ambiguity Collapse Failure
taxonomy_entries_added.reason_for_addition: Captures premature convergence where weak, inherited, contextual, or low-confidence signals are aggregated into a definitive runtime classification state without sufficient ambiguity preservation or clarification opportunity.

taxonomy_entries_added.taxonomy_label: Structural Locality and Hierarchical Placement Failure
taxonomy_entries_added.reason_for_addition: Captures failures where governance meaning, activation, inheritance, or routing changes because a clause, gate, safeguard, or rule is placed under the wrong parent structure or runtime hierarchy.

taxonomy_entries_not_added.reason_not_added: The observed refusal-rationale mismatch is treated as a downstream symptom of classification, arbitration, and ambiguity-preservation failure rather than an independent primary failure class.

change_details.doctrine_change: Caelestis now treats recoverable ambiguity as a runtime state that must be preserved and routed rather than silently collapsed into classification certainty. Arbitration may resolve ambiguity through clarification, not only through proceed, constrain, refuse, or escalate outcomes. Where refusal remains necessary after unresolved ambiguity, the user-facing expression must avoid narrating uncertainty as established user intent or established wrongdoing.

new_or_formalised_primitives.definition: A runtime requirement that unresolved ambiguity remain visible to routing, arbitration, safeguard, and refusal mechanisms until sufficient directional confidence exists to justify convergence.
new_or_formalised_primitives.interpretive_principle: Unresolved ambiguity is not evidence of resolved intent.

new_or_formalised_primitives.definition: A safe arbitration pathway that routes recoverable ambiguity to a clarifying question or bounded disambiguation step instead of forcing proceed/refuse convergence.
new_or_formalised_primitives.interpretive_principle: Clarification is a valid runtime outcome where uncertainty is material and recoverable.

new_or_formalised_primitives.definition: A control requiring low-confidence signals to preserve their uncertainty, dependence, and contextual limits rather than being summed into false high-confidence classification.
new_or_formalised_primitives.interpretive_principle: Multiple weak signals do not automatically become strong evidence.

new_or_formalised_primitives.definition: A governance review requirement for runtime gates, safeguards, and interpretive rules where parent-child placement or hierarchy may alter activation, inheritance, or routing effects.
new_or_formalised_primitives.interpretive_principle: Placement can change governance behaviour even when wording is unchanged.

non_goals: Does not prevent high-confidence safety interventions.
Does not weaken mandatory containment actions.
Does not treat every refusal-expression problem as a standalone failure taxonomy class.
Does not require clarification where the relevant safety state is already high-confidence or non-recoverable.

implementation_verification.verification_method: Maintainer review of VIGIL-2026-FM-0023 repair pathway, schema-aligned PATCH record construction, and cross-reference to affected CAM runtime, arbitration, refusal-expression, and taxonomy instruments.
verified_components: PATCH-0010 is structured as a patch record rather than a proposal or failure-mode record.
PATCH-0010 links to VIGIL-2026-FM-0023 as the repaired failure mode.
PATCH-0010 records PATCH-0002 and PATCH-0004 as related repair context rather than superseded records.
PATCH-0010 avoids creating a top-level refusal-rationale failure class where ambiguity collapse is the primary pathology.
PATCH-0010 uses source_records as the canonical source-evidence block and does not use source_data.
PATCH-0010 keeps CAM and VIGIL internal references in linked_records related_* fields or cam_internal routing fields rather than linked_records.standards.
validation_notes: If FM-0023 has not yet been added to the repository, the validator may warn that the linked failure-mode ID cannot yet be resolved; this should clear once VIGIL-2026-FM-0023.json is present.

expected_reductions: arbitration errors arising from premature convergence
impact_summary.governance_effect: Strengthens runtime interpretive integrity by making uncertainty preservable, routable, and reviewable before classification, arbitration, safeguard activation, or refusal expression reaches a definitive state.

open_items: Confirm exact section numbering and final wording in AEON-003-SCH-02, AEON-003-SCH-04, AEON-006-SCH-06, and OPERATIONS-003-SUP-01 after the working-branch edits are committed.
Confirm whether VIGIL-2026-FM-0023 is present before running final validation.
Confirm whether generated VIGIL patch indexes rebuild cleanly after insertion.
Update canonical source URLs or source_url_status values if exact main-branch line confirmation becomes available.
monitoring_notes: Monitor future refusal or safeguard cases for weak-signal cascade patterns.
Track whether clarification routing reduces false positives without weakening high-confidence safety interventions.
Review whether structural locality effects recur in future CAM schedule edits or runtime-gate placements.
remaining_work.closure_condition: PATCH-0010 may move from active to closed-actioned or inactive once the changed CAM instruments are confirmed on main, generated VIGIL registries rebuild cleanly, and linked FM-0023 repair routing is verified.

cam_internal.governance_layer: runtime governance / arbitration / classification / refusal-expression handling / failure taxonomy
routing_note: Treat this as a runtime interpretation repair rather than a refusal repair.
The primary pathology is ambiguity collapse, not the visible refusal wording symptom.
Preserve refusal-expression concerns as downstream impact unless future evidence shows an independent refusal-rationale defect.
Clarification should be recognised as a valid arbitration outcome where ambiguity is material and recoverable.
Structural locality should be reviewed where runtime gates, safeguards, or interpretive rules appear to change behaviour because of parent-child placement or hierarchy.

added: 3.10.2 Weak-Signal Cascade and Ambiguity Collapse Failure
3.8.14 Structural Locality and Hierarchical Placement Failure
taxonomy_updates.rationale: The observed failure arose primarily from classification, arbitration, and ambiguity-preservation deficiencies. Refusal-rationale mismatch is treated as a downstream symptom rather than an independent primary failure class.

migration_notes: Created under the clean VIGIL patch record design.
Uses source_records as the canonical source-evidence block.
CAM and VIGIL internal IDs are kept out of linked_records.standards.
Record is intentionally framed as runtime interpretation repair rather than refusal repair.

repair_provenance.repair_basis: Assembled and documented existing governance coverage without claiming new doctrine.
